case.title: ABLEMAN v. CITY OF CEDAR RAPIDS

opinion.h1: ABLEMAN v. CITY OF CEDAR RAPIDS, 368 U.S. 3 (1961)
opinion.h4: ABLEMAN ET AL. v. CITY OF CEDAR RAPIDS. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF IOWA. No. 246. Decided October 9, 1961.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Ernest F. Pence and Roy A. Golden for appellants.
C. W. Garberson and William M. Dallas for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: SECURITY-1ST NAT. BK., LOS ANGELES v. FRANCHISE TAX BD., 368 U.S. 3 (1961)
opinion.p: 368 U.S. 3 SECURITY-FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF LOS ANGELES ET AL. v. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD OF CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF CALIFORNIA. No. 122. Decided October 9, 1961.
Pierce Works, Warren M. Christopher, Hugo A. Steinmeyer, Richard E. Sherwood and Brenton L. Metzler for appellants.
Stanley Mosk, Attorney General of California, James E. Sabine, Assistant Attorney General, and Ernest P. Goodman, Deputy Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
